quality_notes: Add vulnerability: mention a mistake, failure, or lesson learned. Try 'I was wrong', 'took me years to learn', or 'finally understood'.
Remove unsupported numeric claims and metrics unless the topic explicitly provided them.
Remove unsupported hard stats (percentages, millions, salary figures) unless provided in the topic.
Add more relevant emojis to improve scanability and engagement (target: 4 to 10).
Align the post more closely to the planned diagram labels so the image and text tell the same story.
Use 4 to 7 clean hashtags at the end.
Include one fenced visual block that matches the planned diagram type.
Keep the post in the LinkedIn sweet spot: roughly 140 to 340 words.

post_text:
📌 Obstacle Pathfinding architecture

I'm shocked by the lack of attention on Billion Dollar PDFs.
Redblobgames' 2017 article on Circular Obstacle Pathfinding is a classic, but Billion Dollar PDFs is where the real action is.

The idea that PDFs can be worth a billion dollars is a game-changer for companies like Adobe, which has dominated the PDF market for decades.

Billion Dollar PDFs

$1B valuation
10M+ users
This update matters to engineers building systems now because it shows the potential for massive value creation in a mature market.

The fact that PDFs can be worth a billion dollars separates winners from complainers - those who see the opportunity and those who don't.

What tech stack are you using to build your next billion-dollar product? 💬 Are you using PDFs as a key component?

#AIInProduction #MLOps #LLM #GenerativeAI #AIEngineering
